q.en: Where does the boy telling the story live?
q.zh: 讲故事的这个男孩住在哪里？

options.en: Seattle, Washington (in the inner city)
options.zh: 华盛顿州的西雅图（市内城区）

options.en: Los Angeles, California
options.zh: 加利福尼亚州的洛杉矶

options.en: A forest in the Northwest
options.zh: 西北部的一片森林

options.en: New York City
options.zh: 纽约市

explain.en: The narrator says, 'I live in Seattle, Washington... the inner city,' and jokes that his cousin from Los Angeles thought everyone in the Northwest lives in the forest.
explain.zh: 叙述者说：「我住在华盛顿州的西雅图……市内城区」，还开玩笑说从洛杉矶来的表亲以为西北部的人都住在森林里。

q.en: What advice does Skimmy, the nurse, give Shaq?
q.zh: 护士 Skimmy 给了 Shaq 什么建议？

options.en: Take advantage of what he is—he is fast, 'speedier than a meteor'—and get faster
options.zh: 发挥自己的长处——他跑得快，「比流星还快」——而且要跑得更快

options.en: Practice shooting every day until he can score
options.zh: 每天练习投篮，直到能得分

options.en: Ask his dad to coach the team
options.zh: 请爸爸来当球队教练

options.en: Quit basketball and try a different sport
options.zh: 放弃篮球，改练别的运动

explain.en: After Shaq's twelfth nosebleed, Skimmy says she watches him play and he gets hurt; she tells him, 'you are fast. You are speedier than a meteor. Why don't you get faster?'
explain.zh: 在 Shaq 第十二次流鼻血后，Skimmy 说他打球总是受伤；她告诉他：「你跑得快。你比流星还快。你为什么不跑得更快呢？」

q.en: What new rule forces team captain Louis Bidwell to put Shaq into the game?
q.zh: 什么新规则迫使队长 Louis Bidwell 让 Shaq 上场？

options.en: A rule that everyone must play in every game
options.zh: 每场比赛每个人都必须上场的规则

options.en: A rule that the tallest player must start
options.zh: 最高的球员必须首发的规则

options.en: A rule that games last only four minutes
options.zh: 比赛只打四分钟的规则

options.en: A rule that only girls can play
options.zh: 只有女孩能上场的规则

explain.en: The book says there was 'a new rule this year that everybody got to play in every game,' so with the team down six points and four minutes left, Louis had to put Shaq in or forfeit.
explain.zh: 书里说「今年有了一条新规则：每场比赛每个人都必须上场」，所以球队落后六分、还剩四分钟时，Louis 必须让 Shaq 上场，否则就要弃权。

q.en: From what point of view is the story told?
q.zh: 这个故事是用什么视角（人称）叙述的？

options.en: First person—the narrator says 'I live in Seattle' and 'My name is Shaquille O'Neal'
options.zh: 第一人称——叙述者说「我住在西雅图」「我的名字叫 Shaquille O'Neal」

options.en: Third person—a narrator describes the boy from outside
options.zh: 第三人称——由外部的叙述者描写这个男孩

options.en: Second person—the reader is addressed as 'you'
options.zh: 第二人称——以「你」来称呼读者

options.en: It is written as a play script
options.zh: 它是一出剧本

explain.en: The story opens with 'I live in Seattle, Washington' and 'My name is Shaquille O'Neal,' so the narrator is telling his own story in first person.
explain.zh: 故事开头是「我住在华盛顿州的西雅图」和「我的名字叫 Shaquille O'Neal」，所以叙述者是用第一人称讲自己的故事。

q.en: Why is the book titled 'Speedier than a Meteor'?
q.zh: 这本书为什么叫「比流星还快」？

options.en: It echoes Skimmy's words praising how fast Shaq becomes after he trains hard
options.zh: 它呼应了 Skimmy 的话，称赞 Shaq 努力训练后变得多么快

options.en: It is the nickname of the famous basketball player Shaquille O'Neal
options.zh: 那是著名篮球运动员 Shaquille O'Neal 的绰号

options.en: It describes a real meteor that falls in the story
options.zh: 它描写了故事里一颗真的流星

options.en: It is the name of Shaq's basketball team
options.zh: 那是 Shaq 所在球队的名字

explain.en: Skimmy tells Shaq, 'You are speedier than a meteor,' and after training all summer he agrees, 'I really am speedier than a meteor'—the title captures his new speed.
explain.zh: Skimmy 对 Shaq 说「你比流星还快」，整个夏天训练后他也承认「我真的比流星还快」——书名正是抓住了他的新速度。

q.en: Even though Shaq helps his team win, why does he never score by shooting?
q.zh: 虽然 Shaq 帮球队赢了，为什么他从不用投篮得分？

options.en: He practiced running and dribbling but never practiced shooting
options.zh: 他练了跑步和运球，却从没练过投篮

options.en: He was too short to reach the basket
options.zh: 他太矮了，够不到篮筐

options.en: The coach banned him from shooting
options.zh: 教练禁止他投篮

options.en: He forgot to bring a basketball
options.zh: 他忘记带篮球

explain.en: After the game Dad asks why he never shot; Shaq says he practiced running and dribbling and 'nobody can dribble better than I can,' but 'I never practiced shooting, and I'm still rotten at that.'
explain.zh: 赛后爸爸问他为什么不投篮；Shaq 说他练了跑步和运球，「没人比我运球更好」，但「我从没练过投篮，那方面还是糟透了」。

q.en: How does Shaq's speed training help his team win the game?
q.zh: Shaq 的速度训练怎样帮助球队获胜？

options.en: He is fast enough to steal the ball, race down the court, and pass to Louis, who scores
options.zh: 他快得能断球、冲向对方篮下，并把球传给 Louis 让他得分

options.en: He learns to shoot three-pointers from far away
options.zh: 他学会了从远处投三分球

options.en: He becomes the team captain
options.zh: 他当上了队长

options.en: He teaches Louis how to dribble
options.zh: 他教 Louis 如何运球

explain.en: Shaq slips in fast, 'stripped the ball away,' dribbles the other way, and passes hard to Louis, who shoots 'swish, all net'—because of Shaq, 'we won!'
explain.zh: Shaq 飞快插进去，「把球断了下来」，运球往回跑，用力传给 Louis，Louis 投出「唰的一声，空心入网」——「因为我们，赢了！」

q.en: The book's Focus Question asks how Shaq changes after talking with Skimmy. What change happens?
q.zh: 书的「聚焦问题」问 Shaq 和 Skimmy 谈话后有了什么变化。发生了什么改变？

options.en: He stops just sitting out and instead trains hard, using his speed to become a real part of the team
options.zh: 他不再只是坐冷板凳，而是刻苦训练，用速度成了球队真正的一员

options.en: He decides to become a nurse like Skimmy
options.zh: 他决定像 Skimmy 一样去当护士

options.en: He stops liking basketball
options.zh: 他不再喜欢篮球了

options.en: He moves to Los Angeles
options.zh: 他搬到了洛杉矶

explain.en: At first Shaq is 'chosen last' and just sits while others play; after Skimmy's advice he 'never walked anyplace—I ran' and dribbled everywhere, becoming fast and useful.
explain.zh: 起初 Shaq「最后一个被选中」、别人打球他只能坐着；听了 Skimmy 的建议后，他「哪儿都不走——我跑」，到处运球，变得又快又有用。

q.en: What main message does the author share at the end of the book?
q.zh: 作者在书的结尾传达了什么主要信息？

options.en: Practice leads to improvement ('Without practice nothing gets perfect') and using your own strengths matters
options.zh: 练习带来进步（「没有练习就没有完美」），并且发挥自己的长处很重要

options.en: Only tall people can play basketball well
options.zh: 只有高个子才能打好篮球

options.en: Rich people are always better at sports
options.zh: 有钱人运动总是更好

options.en: You should never trust strangers
options.zh: 你永远不该相信陌生人

explain.en: The last page says, 'Without practice nothing gets perfect—just ask the real Shaq about his free throw shooting,' showing the author wants readers to value practice and self-improvement.
explain.zh: 最后一页写道：「没有练习就没有完美——去问问真正的 Shaq 他的罚球吧」，说明作者希望读者重视练习和自我提升。

q.en: In the final winning play, what does Shaq do with the ball?
q.zh: 在最后制胜的一球中，Shaq 对球做了什么？

options.en: He steals it, dribbles down the court, and passes to Louis, who scores
options.zh: 他断下球，运球冲向对方篮下，传给 Louis 让他得分

options.en: He shoots a basket himself
options.zh: 他自己投进了一球

options.en: He blocks the other team's shot
options.zh: 他盖了对方的帽

options.en: He argues with the referee
options.zh: 他和裁判争吵

explain.en: When the other team comes down the court, Shaq 'slipped in there fast and stripped the ball away,' then dribbled and passed to Louis, who scored the winning basket.
explain.zh: 对方进攻时，Shaq「飞快插进去把球断了下来」，然后运球并传给 Louis，由 Louis 投进了制胜一球。

prompt.en: Shaq is small and not a good shooter, but he becomes a hero by using his speed—something he could control through practice. Think of a time you improved at something by practicing, or when you used one of your strengths to help a group. What did you learn about effort and using what you have?
prompt.zh: Shaq 个子小、投篮差，却靠速度成了英雄——而速度是他通过练习可以掌控的。回想一次你通过练习而进步的经历，或者你用自己的某个长处帮了团队的事。关于努力和「发挥自己拥有的长处」，你学到了什么？

en: Skimmy tells Shaq to 'take advantage of what you are' because he is fast (p. 10-11).
All summer he 'ran and dribbled and dribbled and ran' until he got 'faster and faster and better and better' (p. 12).
A good answer gives a real example of practice or using a strength, and explains what it taught you.
zh: Skimmy 让 Shaq「发挥你自己的长处」，因为他跑得快（第10-11页）。
整个夏天他「跑啊运球、运球啊跑」，直到变得「越来越快、越来越好」（第12页）。
好的回答会举一个真实的练习或发挥长处的例子，并说明它让你学到了什么。
